title: sanguisorba officinalis var. serotina
intro: Sanguisorba officinalis var. serotina, also known as the red stemmed sanguisorba, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its red-stemmed flowers and leaves that resemble those of the strawberry plant. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant's roots and leaves have been found to possess astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sanguisorba officinalis var. serotina include its ability to treat diarrhea, dysentery, and hemorrhages due to its tannin content, which can help to stop bleeding and reduce inflammation. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in salves and poultices to support wound healing and reduce inflammation.
